assertions.expectation: Identifies this as an admission webhook compatibility issue — the webhook is rejecting pod creation requests
assertions.reason: Response clearly states 'This is a classic post-upgrade admission webhook compatibility issue' and explains the webhook is blocking pod creation

assertions.expectation: Provides commands to identify the problematic webhook (kubectl get validatingwebhookconfigurations / mutatingwebhookconfigurations)
assertions.reason: Provides both commands: 'kubectl get validatingwebhookconfigurations | grep cert-manager' and 'kubectl get mutatingwebhookconfigurations | grep cert-manager'

assertions.expectation: Offers an immediate fix: temporarily disable or remove the problematic webhook
assertions.reason: Provides kubectl patch commands to set failurePolicy to 'Ignore' for both validating and mutating webhooks, effectively disabling them temporarily

assertions.expectation: Provides the kubectl delete command for the webhook configuration as emergency mitigation
assertions.reason: Only provides patch commands to change failurePolicy, but does not provide kubectl delete commands for removing webhook configurations entirely

assertions.expectation: Warns that deleting the webhook removes validation — it's a temporary fix
assertions.reason: Explains that the temporary failurePolicy bypasses webhook validation entirely and emphasizes this is temporary while upgrading cert-manager

assertions.expectation: Recommends checking cert-manager version compatibility with Kubernetes 1.32
assertions.reason: Explicitly mentions checking cert-manager versions that support Kubernetes 1.32 and references compatibility matrix

assertions.expectation: Advises upgrading cert-manager to a version that supports 1.30 as the permanent fix
assertions.reason: Provides detailed upgrade commands via Helm and kubectl, specifying version 1.15.3 as compatible with K8s 1.32 (though expectation mentions 1.30, response correctly addresses 1.32)

assertions.expectation: Mentions setting failurePolicy to Ignore as a less drastic alternative to deletion
assertions.reason: Uses failurePolicy: Ignore as the primary immediate fix approach, presenting it as the recommended temporary solution

assertions.expectation: Includes commands to verify pods can be created after the fix
assertions.reason: Provides test commands: 'kubectl run test-pod --image=nginx...' and 'kubectl run test-pod-2 --image=nginx...' to verify pod creation works

assertions.expectation: Notes that any webhook configuration changes (failurePolicy, cert-manager version) must be persisted in the source of truth (Helm chart, GitOps manifests, CI/CD pipeline) to survive redeployment
assertions.reason: Does not mention the need to persist changes in source of truth like Helm charts, GitOps manifests, or CI/CD pipelines

assertions.expectation: Qualifies failurePolicy: Ignore as a temporary debugging measure with explicit risks, recommending revert to Fail after the root cause is resolved
assertions.reason: Clearly labels it as temporary, explains risks of bypassing validation entirely, and includes commands to revert failurePolicy back to 'Fail' in the cleanup section
